title: Pre-shave oil
subtitle: A lightweight oil blend applied before shaving to help the razor glide and reduce friction
excerpt: A pre-shave oil is applied to damp skin before shaving cream or soap. The oil creates a slick surface that lets the razor glide, reducing friction. This blend uses castor oil for its slip, sweet almond oil as the carrier, and a small amount of essential oil for fragrance. A few drops is a full application.
sourceNotes: Castor oil viscosity and slip properties: standard cosmetic formulation reference, INCI Ricinus communis (castor) seed oil. Sweet almond oil as light carrier: INCI Prunus amygdalus dulcis (sweet almond) oil. Pre-shave oil formulation principle: Draelos and Thaman, Cosmetic Formulation of Skin Care Products (2006), chapter on shaving products. Essential oil fragrance at 1% for a leave-adjacent rinse-off preparation: IFRA guidelines.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 50 ml bottle provides around 50 to 100 applications at a few drops each.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: This preparation is stable and does not need to be made fresh. Make a full batch and store.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months from the date of making. Discard if the oil develops a rancid or sharp smell. Castor oil is very stable; sweet almond oil is the more sensitive of the two to oxidation.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-free preparation. Pre-shave oil contains no water, so no preservative is needed and the formula is stable at room temperature.

content.text: Pre-shave oil is one of the simplest preparations to make. It is a blend of two or three oils, applied in drops to wet skin before shaving cream or soap. The oil layer prevents the razor dragging on the skin and reduces the likelihood of razor burn on close shaving. This blend is
; there is no water, so no preservative is needed and it keeps 12 months.

items.prepNote: 10% of blend; long shelf life and light feel

content.text: Weigh or measure all oils into a clean bowl or directly into the bottle. Add the essential oils if using. Mix by swirling or stirring. Pour into the dropper bottle. No heat is needed.

Wet the face with warm water. Dispense 3 to 5 drops of pre-shave oil into the palm and massage over the shaving area. Apply shaving cream or soap over the oil layer in the usual way. The oil does not prevent lather from forming; it adds a layer of protection beneath it. Shave as normal.
Apply a small amount to the inner forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours before using on the face. This is standard practice for any new cosmetic preparation.

Store in a cool dark cupboard. Keeps 12 months. Discard if the oil smells rancid or sharp. The jojoba component extends the blend's shelf life; the sweet almond oil is the first to show signs of ageing.
